message:
fix(messaging): incremental stream retention and dead-pattern removal

Stream retention (stream.rs):
  The retention enforcer previously rebuilt the full state (msg_count,
  byte_count, first_seq, last_seq) by snapshotting all records after
  every single append. This was O(n) per append where n is the number
  of stored records — catastrophic for high-throughput streams.

  The fix makes retention tracking incremental:
  - append() now updates state counters directly (msg_count, byte_count,
    first_seq, last_seq) after writing to storage
  - enforce_retention() decrements counters per removed record and prunes
    the dedup index entry-by-entry, avoiding full snapshot scans
  - A resync_from_storage() fallback detects when incremental counters
    drift from reality (e.g. after a crash) and rebuilds once, then
    retries. A boolean guard prevents infinite resync loops.
  - Removed the unused StreamState::new() constructor and the per-append
    rebuild_state() call

  The net effect is O(1) per append (amortized) instead of O(n), with
  self-healing if the incremental state ever diverges.

Capability pattern matching (capability/mod.rs):
  Removed unreachable match arm (SubjectToken::Tail, None) — a Tail
  token in the granted pattern with an empty requested pattern is
  already handled by the (SubjectToken::Tail, Some(_)) | (None, None)
  arm returning true. The Tail-vs-empty case would never be reached.

Kafka consumer (kafka_consumer.rs):
  Replaced `.iter().cloned().collect()` with `.to_vec()` — the
  assignments parameter is already a slice, so to_vec() is clearer
  and avoids the intermediate iterator adapter.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -356,7 +356,6 @@ fn pattern_covers_segments(granted: &[SubjectToken], requested: &[SubjectToken])
     match (granted.split_first(), requested.split_first()) {
         (Some((SubjectToken::Tail, _)), Some(_)) | (None, None) => true,
         (None, Some(_))
-        | (Some((SubjectToken::Tail, _)), None)
         | (Some(_), None)
         | (
             Some((SubjectToken::Literal(_), _)),
